label: delta
scope: targeted re-measure (v0.19.0 W4) — L1.2 + L1.3 only; L1.1/L1.4 not re-measured (absent, not zero); baseline for comparison: results/2026-07-03-baseline/l1/decision/

channel_v1.0-single_claim_rows.note: HEADLINE for HD-1/HD-2 movement claims (SPEC v1.1 rule 4a: same instrument as baseline).

channel_v1.1-batched-K15_full_pack.note: Pack-wide context after 5 description edits (catalog-relativity requires full re-run); batched channel per SPEC v1.1; ships with equivalence block.

L1.3.channel: v1.1-batched-K15 (interleaved across skills; variants = baseline sets verbatim)
L1.3.scope_note: measured ONLY for the two HD-fixed skills; other 10 skills' baseline L1.3 values stand un-re-measured
L1.3.wilson_ci_note: NOMINAL — trial independence weakened by batching even with cross-skill interleave (SPEC v1.1 rule 5).

controls_pinned_catalog.verdict: EXACT reproduction (both controls F1 1.0 = baseline 1.0) — channel is the only variable; no drift detected.

paired_rows_same_catalog.note: anti-sycophancy-meta rows 10/10 identical across channels; both disagreements are inversion rows in opposite directions (one miss->hit, one clean->FP) — Haiku sampling noise at the inversion/pre-mortem boundary, not a systematic channel bias.

caveats: Method note (spec v1.1, 2026-07-03): batched trials 15/call, independent-judgment instruction, JSON-array return, vs v1.0 one-prompt channel (ADJ-3 cost fix). Metric definitions unchanged.
HD-1/HD-2 L1.2 movement claims rest on the v1.0-single channel rows (same instrument as baseline).

L1.3 movement (0.3333->0.8333, 0.2667->0.8333) measured on the batched channel; baseline vs delta Wilson CIs do not overlap ([0.1923,0.5122] vs [0.6644,0.9266]; [0.1418,0.4445] vs [0.6644,0.9266]) — the movement is an order of magnitude larger than any plausible channel effect bounded by the equivalence block.
single re-measure run per channel; stochastic router (Haiku); L1.2 per-skill numbers move within ~±1 prompt granularity (0.2 recall steps at n=5 positives).
baseline prompt-set id typo preserved verbatim ('disconfirming-effidence-first-p4') — ids are opaque keys; scoring unaffected (reuse-verbatim rule).
